action.reasoning: Observation: All training and adapter attempts have failed to improve over the pretrained MetricGAN+ baseline (test PESQ 3.134). Post-processing (alpha mixing, RMS matching, high-pass, pre-emphasis) and inference changes (bs=1) provided no test improvement. With 7 iterations and ~3.5h left, I need a zero-risk inference tweak. Analysis: A common zero-cost trick for iterative mask-based enhancers is double-pass inference: run the model on its own output. This can suppress residual musical noise. It is trivial to test on the local validation set. If validation PESQ improves, it's a guaranteed safe win. Decision: Write a quick script to evaluate double-pass and double-pass+mixing on the full validation set. If any variant improves val PESQ over the raw 2.8490 baseline, update predict.py immediately and submit eval.
